title: Jesus Desperate to Reveal Himself to His Family
summary: “Then Joseph could not refrain himself before all them that stood by him; and he cried, Cause every man to go out from me. And there stood no man with him, while Joseph made himself known unto his brethren.” Genesis 45:1. We know the background to this incredible story. Joseph’s brothers had first tried to kill him and then settled on selling him to some Ishmaelite slave traders who took him to Egypt. Joseph, however, became the second most powerful ruler in Egypt because God was with him. Spiritually speaking, our sins are the brothers of Joseph. Every one of us is a Reuben, Simeon, Judah, Levi, Zebulon, etc. It was our sins that led our Lord to the Cross of Calvary. “But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed” (Isaiah 53: 5). Our sins were the reason the Lord Jesus ended up on earth just as the sins of Joseph’s brothers was the reason he ended up in a foreign country, Egypt. Jesus said he was not of this world, and Joseph’s heart was never comfortable in Egypt.
